findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
findings.code_excerpt:
next_page = request.args.get('next')
            return redirect(url_for(next_page) if next_page else url_for('dashboard'))

tool_specific.lines:
next_page = request.args.get('next')
            return redirect(url_for(next_page) if next_page else url_for('dashboard'))

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-default-name
findings.description: Don’t use the default session cookie name Using the default session cookie name can open your app to attacks. The security issue posed is similar to X-Powered-By: a potential attacker can use it to fingerprint the server and target attacks accordingly.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-no-domain
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `domain` not set. It indicates the domain of the cookie; use it to compare against the domain of the server in which the URL is being requested. If they match, then check the path attribute next.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-no-expires
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `expires` not set. Use it to set expiration date for persistent cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-no-httponly
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `httpOnly` not set. It ensures the cookie is sent only over HTTP(S), not client JavaScript, helping to protect against cross-site scripting attacks.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-no-path
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `path` not set. It indicates the path of the cookie; use it to compare against the request path. If this and domain match, then send the cookie in the request.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-session-no-secure
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `secure` not set. It ensures the browser only sends the cookie over HTTPS.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-session-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'check_call' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-tainted-env-args
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'check_call' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.quote()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.missing-template-string-indicator
findings.description: This looks like a JavaScript template string. Are you missing a '$' in front of '{...}'?
findings.code_excerpt:
const REDEEM_SCRIPT = `
  local key          = KEYS[1]
  local userId       = ARGV[1]
  local now          = tonumber(ARGV[2])
  local orderTotal   = tonumber(ARGV[3])

  if redis.call('EXISTS', key) == 0 then
    return {0, 'INVALID_CODE'}
  end

  if redis.call('HGET', key, 'used') == '1' then
    return {0, 'ALREADY_USED'}
  end

  local expiresAt = redis.call('HGET', key, 'expiresAt')
  if expiresAt ~= false and expiresAt ~= '' and tonumber(expiresAt) < now then
    return {0, 'EXPIRED'}
  end

  redis.call('HSET', key, 'used', '1', 'usedBy', userId, 'usedAt', now)

  local discountType  = redis.call('HGET', key, 'discountType')
  local discountValue = tonumber(redis.call('HGET', key, 'discountValue'))

  local discount = 0
  if discountType == 'percent' then
    discount = math.floor(orderTotal * discountValue / 100)
  else
    discount = discountValue
  end
  if discount > orderTotal then
    discount = orderTotal
  end

  return {1, tostring(discount)}
`;

$STR.abstract_content:
``

  local userId       = ARGV[1]
  local now          = tonumber(ARGV[2])
  local orderTotal   = tonumber(ARGV[3])

    return {0, 'INVALID_CODE'}
  end

  if redis.call('HGET', key, 'used') == '1' then

  end

    return {0, 'EXPIRED'}
  end

  local discountType  = redis.call('HGET', key, 'discountType')
  local discountValue = tonumber(redis.call('HGET', key, 'discountValue'))

  local discount = 0
  if discountType == 'percent' then
    discount = math.floor(orderTotal * discountValue / 100)
  else
    discount = discountValue
  end
  if discount > orderTotal then
    discount = orderTotal
  end

  return {1, tostring(discount)}
`

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string
findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
return render_template_string('''
        <form method="post">
            <input name="username" placeholder="Username">
            <button type="submit">Login</button>
        </form>
    ''')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
findings.description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
